title: Wholesome Irish Walnut Salad
description: A winey, crunchy Irish salad featuring caramelized onions, fresh birds-eye chillies, and toasted walnuts, crafted to be sattvic-friendly and delightfully light.
instructions: Place the mixed leafy greens in a large bowl and set aside.
Thinly slice the birds-eye chillies, removing seeds if desired, and add them to the greens.
Slice the red onion thinly and place in a small pan with 1 tbsp olive oil over medium heat.
Cook the onion until softened and lightly caramelized, about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the onion cooks, toast the walnuts in a dry skillet until fragrant, about 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
In a small bowl, whisk together the white wine, apple cider vinegar, dried mustard, maple syrup, remaining olive oil, sea salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Once the onion is caramelized, remove the pan from heat and let it cool slightly before adding to the bowl with greens and chillies.
Add the toasted walnuts, caramelized onion, and apple slices to the greens, then pour the dressing over the salad.
Toss gently until everything is evenly coated with dressing.
Divide the salad into two portions and serve immediately for a light, winey crunch.
